subject: UI/UX Design Intelligence — UX Flow Design & Information Architecture

clusters.name: Information Architecture & Research Methods
clusters.summary: Card sorting and tree testing methodologies for discovering and validating navigation structures. Mental models and cognitive load theory as foundations for IA decisions.

clusters.name: Navigation Taxonomy & Structural Patterns
clusters.summary: Flat vs deep, hub-and-spoke, faceted, and linear navigation patterns. Material Design and Apple HIG guidelines for choosing navigation structures.

clusters.name: Navigation Component Patterns
clusters.summary: Breadcrumb types and usage rules, mobile navigation patterns (tab bars, hamburger, bottom sheets, gestures), and responsive adaptation strategies.

clusters.name: Onboarding Flow Patterns & PLG
clusters.summary: Five onboarding pattern types (checklists, tooltips, tours, progressive, empty-state), PLG metrics, retention/conversion data, and pattern selection framework.

clusters.name: Wizard & Multi-Step Form Patterns
clusters.summary: Wizard UI design principles: step count, branching logic, progress indication, validation, mobile optimization, and completion rate factors.

clusters.name: Command Palette & Search-as-Navigation
clusters.summary: Cmd+K pattern: history, variations (universal/contextual/recent-first), implementation libraries (cmdk ecosystem), accessibility requirements, and fuzzy search design.

clusters.name: Progressive Disclosure & Complexity Management
clusters.summary: Two-level disclosure principles, 30-50% faster initial completion, Carroll/Rosson IBM research, distinction from staged disclosure (wizards).

clusters.name: Dashboard Design & Information Hierarchy
clusters.summary: 5-second comprehension rule, KPI placement, widget customization, AI personalization, minimalist trends, alert management, and accessibility.

clusters.name: Error Flows & Recovery Patterns
clusters.summary: Five error types with specific recovery flows, Nielsen's Recognize-Diagnose-Recover framework, session expiry handling, network error resilience, 404 page design.

clusters.name: Zero-Data & Empty State Design
clusters.summary: Three empty state types (no-data, user-action, error-management), Carbon Design System anatomy, positive framing, starter content strategy, accessibility.

clusters.name: Notification Architecture & Attention Management
clusters.summary: Notification types and channels, badge count synchronization, inbox pattern for persistent browsable notifications, notification center design, user preference management.

clusters.name: Settings Page Architecture
clusters.summary: Six layout types, search-in-settings (5x improvement), category limits, component patterns (toggles, dropdowns, sliders), mobile adaptation, progressive disclosure.

clusters.name: Deep Linking & State Preservation
clusters.summary: URL-as-state pattern, shareable state via URL parameters, browser history integration, synthetic back stacks, React implementation approaches.

clusters.name: Implementation Libraries & Tools
clusters.summary: React ecosystem libraries for command palettes (cmdk), onboarding tours (Joyride, Shepherd, Onborda), and wizard/stepper components (MUI, rhf-wizard).

gaps: Limited empirical data on faceted navigation effectiveness in SaaS vs ecommerce contexts
No quantitative studies found on settings page search adoption rates
Deep linking patterns for SPAs with complex state machines need more coverage
Notification fatigue thresholds and optimal frequency data is sparse

agent_generation_assessment.reason: 14 clusters with 135+ actionable tactics spanning IA research methods, navigation design, onboarding patterns, error flows, and implementation libraries. Sufficient depth for a UI/UX flow design specialist agent.
